article_title: HackerOne Discloses Employee Data Breach After Third-Party Benefits Provider Navia Exposes 2.7 Million Records

content_preview.title: HackerOne Discloses Employee Data Breach After Third-Party Benefits Provider Navia Exposes 2.7 Million Records
content_preview.summary: Bug bounty platform HackerOne confirms 287 employees had Social Security numbers and personal data exposed through a BOLA vulnerability at benefits administrator Navia, part of a broader breach affecting 2.7 million people.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

HackerOne, the San Francisco-based bug bounty and vulnerability coordination platform, has disclosed that 287 of its employees had sensitive personal data exposed in a breach at third-party benefits administrator Navia Benefit Solutions. The compromise, which ran from December 22, 2025 through January 15, 2026, exploited a Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA) vulnerability in Navia's systems and affected approximately 2.7 million individuals across Navia's client base, according...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article with clear sections covering the breach details, notification delay controversy, and supply chain security analysis. 618 words appropriate for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified. BleepingComputer confirmed: 287 employees, BOLA vulnerability, 2.7M total, Dec 22-Jan 15 access window, Jan 23 detection, Feb 20 notification, Maine AG filing. The Register confirmed: notification delay to March, 'still waiting' quote, supply chain irony angle. SecurityWeek confirmed: data types exposed, 'safe handling' quote, no misuse disclaimer, credit monitoring details.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified across three independent sources. Dates, figures, quotes, and technical details all match. BOLA explanation accurate per OWASP API Top 10.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The irony angle is noted factually without editorializing. Good balance between technical and business context.
editor_notes.originality: No prior HackerOne or Navia breach coverage. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent cybersecurity article with all claims verified. Ready for publication.
